name: Cell-death kinetics and membrane integrity
plainEnglish: Measure when cells lose viability and membrane integrity instead of relying on a single endpoint.
measures: The timing and magnitude of death under a defined perturbation.
cannotProve: It does not identify ferroptosis without mechanistic rescue, iron dependence and lipid-oxidation evidence.
bestPractice: Use time-resolved imaging or repeated measurements
Report seeded density, medium, serum, dose and exposure time
Separate growth arrest from loss of membrane integrity
commonPitfalls: One endpoint after extensive secondary damage
Unreported cell density or nutrient conditions

name: Orthogonal rescue and pathway exclusion
plainEnglish: Ask whether chemically different radical-trapping antioxidants and iron chelators rescue death, while inhibitors of other death routes do not explain the phenotype.
measures: Pharmacological dependence of a death phenotype on lipid-radical chemistry and iron availability.
cannotProve: A single compound rescue is not specific enough to name the death mechanism.
bestPractice: Use at least two chemically distinct ferroptosis-suppressing approaches
Include dose-response and non-ferroptotic death controls
Pair rescue with genetics or direct chemistry

name: BODIPY 581/591 C11 oxidation assay
plainEnglish: A membrane-localized fluorescent fatty-acid analogue shifts from red toward green after oxidation; it is usually read by flow cytometry or microscopy.
measures: Oxidation of the probe in its local membrane environment.
cannotProve: It does not identify an endogenous oxidized lipid species and cannot diagnose ferroptosis alone.
bestPractice: Use the oxidized-to-reduced fluorescence ratio
Keep loading, light exposure and acquisition settings constant
Measure before widespread cell rupture
Confirm with rescue and a direct or orthogonal lipid assay
commonPitfalls: Reporting green intensity without the red channel

decisionProfile.provisionalBecause: 9 of 13 decision axes were read from the vendor page, Zou 2020 and Kagan 2017 on 2026-07-24; quantificationUnit, positiveControl, negativeControl and processControl are pending because no opened source establishes them. The parallel H2-DCFDA readout is recorded as orthogonal confirmation, not as a process control.

specimen.value: In the demonstrating study (Zou 2020): UACC-257 melanoma and 786-O / 769-P clear-cell renal carcinoma lines, wild-type against POR-depleted.

evidence.claimFragment: UACC-257 melanoma and 786-O / 769-P clear-cell renal carcinoma lines
evidence.supportNote: The flow and imaging Methods name the cell lines and the wild-type/POR-depleted comparison.

evidence.claimFragment: wild-type against POR-depleted
evidence.supportNote: The flow and imaging Methods state the wild-type against POR-depleted comparison.

question.value: Whether membrane lipid peroxidation rises under a ferroptotic perturbation, read as a shift in probe emission rather than as an identified lipid species.

evidence.claimFragment: read as a shift in probe emission
evidence.supportNote: The vendor page describes the probe as reporting membrane lipid peroxidation via an emission shift.

evidence.claimFragment: membrane lipid peroxidation rises under a ferroptotic perturbation
evidence.supportNote: The results read the probe's emission shift as membrane lipid peroxidation under the ferroptotic perturbation.

evidence.claimFragment: rather than as an identified lipid species
evidence.supportNote: Kagan states that C11-BODIPY, unlike LiperFluo, does not interact with phospholipid hydroperoxides; reading this as a method boundary — an emission shift, not a species identification — is a derivation from that specificity limit, not a claim the assay itself makes.

perturbation.value: The probe is a reporter, not the perturbation; in the demonstrating study the perturbation is GPX4 inhibition by ML210 (5 micromolar) against DMSO, with POR depletion as the genetic arm.

evidence.claimFragment: The probe is a reporter, not the perturbation
evidence.supportNote: The flow Methods state ML210 (5 uM) against DMSO and the POR-depletion arm.

readout.value: Oxidation of the probe's polyunsaturated butadienyl group shifts its fluorescence emission peak from approximately 590 nm (reduced) to approximately 510 nm (oxidised); read by flow cytometry (reduced in the PE-Texas Red channel, oxidised in FITC) or by fluorescence microscopy.

evidence.claimFragment: Oxidation of the probe's polyunsaturated butadienyl group shifts its fluorescence emission peak from approximately 590 nm (reduced) to approximately 510 nm (oxidised)
evidence.supportNote: The vendor page states the reduced-to-oxidised emission shift and its wavelengths.

evidence.claimFragment: read by flow cytometry (reduced in the PE-Texas Red channel, oxidised in FITC)
evidence.supportNote: The flow Methods assign the reduced probe to PE-Texas Red and the oxidised probe to FITC.

quantificationUnit.unresolved: The vendor page states the emission shift qualitatively and Zou 2020's Methods describe channel assignment but do not state the exact quantification unit (e.g. oxidised/reduced ratio or percentage-shifted). Resolving it requires the figure and its legend (Fig. 5b,c) or the version of record, which were not opened.

instrument.value: Fluorescence detection: a flow cytometer (Zou used a SONY SH800) or a fluorescence imager (Zou used a Perkin Elmer Operetta); excitation/emission 581/591 nm for the reduced probe per the vendor specification.

evidence.claimFragment: a flow cytometer (Zou used a SONY SH800) or a fluorescence imager (Zou used a Perkin Elmer Operetta)
evidence.supportNote: The vendor specification gives excitation/emission 581/591 nm for the reduced probe.

evidence.claimFragment: excitation/emission 581/591 nm for the reduced probe per the vendor specification
evidence.supportNote: The flow Methods name the SONY SH800; the imaging Methods name the Operetta.

positiveControl.unresolved: Neither the vendor page nor the Zou 2020 BODIPY sections name a positive oxidation control for the probe itself (e.g. cumene hydroperoxide). Resolving it requires a protocol source that specifies one.

negativeControl.unresolved: The demonstrating study reads the probe against a DMSO condition, but does not describe a radical-trapping-antioxidant control (Fer-1/Lip-1) specifically for the BODIPY readout in the sections read. Resolving it requires the figure legend or a dedicated protocol.

processControl.unresolved: The demonstrating study runs a parallel cytosolic-ROS reporter (H2-DCFDA) alongside BODIPY-C11, but that is an orthogonal specificity comparator, not a control for probe loading, handling, acquisition or assay performance; it is recorded under orthogonalConfirmation instead. No process control for the BODIPY readout is specified in the opened sources. Resolving it requires a protocol that names one.

orthogonalConfirmation.value: In the demonstrating study the probe is read alongside other lipid-peroxidation readouts: a parallel cytosolic-ROS reporter (H2-DCFDA) distinguishes a membrane-lipid change from a general cytosolic ROS rise, and the study also pairs BODIPY-C11 with a malondialdehyde assay and species-resolved redox-lipidomics. The study presents these readouts together; it does not state a rule that they must move together.

evidence.claimFragment: a parallel cytosolic-ROS reporter (H2-DCFDA) distinguishes a membrane-lipid change from a general cytosolic ROS rise
evidence.supportNote: The flow Methods run H2-DCFDA in parallel with BODIPY-C11 as a separate ROS readout.

evidence.claimFragment: The study presents these readouts together
evidence.supportNote: The results present BODIPY-C11 together with H2-DCFDA, MDA and redox-lipidomics, without asserting a universal must-move-together rule.

evidence.claimFragment: the study also pairs BODIPY-C11 with a malondialdehyde assay and species-resolved redox-lipidomics
evidence.supportNote: Fig. 5 shows these readouts side by side.

timing.value: Acute probe loading: 5 micromolar BODIPY-C11 for the final 45 minutes of a 90-minute ML210 treatment for flow cytometry; for imaging, probe added for the last 30 minutes of a 3-hour ML210 exposure.

evidence.claimFragment: 5 micromolar BODIPY-C11 for the final 45 minutes of a 90-minute ML210 treatment for flow cytometry
evidence.supportNote: The flow Methods state 5 uM probe for the final 45 min of a 90-min ML210 treatment.

evidence.claimFragment: for imaging, probe added for the last 30 minutes of a 3-hour ML210 exposure
evidence.supportNote: The imaging Methods state probe added for the last 30 min of a 3-hour ML210 exposure.

compartmentResolution.value: The probe partitions into membranes and reports their local oxidation, but resolves no molecular lipid species; fluorescence imaging gives only coarse subcellular localisation, not organelle-specific chemistry.

evidence.claimFragment: The probe partitions into membranes and reports their local oxidation, but resolves no molecular lipid species
evidence.supportNote: The vendor page describes the probe as a membrane-partitioning reporter with no species resolution.

evidence.claimFragment: fluorescence imaging gives only coarse subcellular localisation, not organelle-specific chemistry
evidence.supportNote: That imaging gives only coarse subcellular localisation is inferred from the probe's membrane partitioning and the imaging setup, not stated as a limitation in the text.

confounders.value: The probe reacts with peroxyl radicals and is not specific to phospholipid hydroperoxides: Kagan 2017 states that both C11-BODIPY and LiperFluo react with peroxyl radicals whereas LiperFluo, but not C11-BODIPY, interacts with (phospho)lipid hydroperoxides, so the probe is a surrogate rather than a direct measure. It is also photosensitive and, per the vendor, is handled in DMSO and stored desiccated at -20 C in the dark.

evidence.claimFragment: The probe reacts with peroxyl radicals and is not specific to phospholipid hydroperoxides
evidence.supportNote: Kagan states verbatim that C11-BODIPY and LiperFluo both react with peroxyl radicals while LiperFluo, but not C11-BODIPY, interacts with (phospho)lipid hydroperoxides.

evidence.claimFragment: It is also photosensitive and, per the vendor, is handled in DMSO and stored desiccated at -20 C in the dark
evidence.supportNote: The vendor page states the probe is photosensitive, handled in DMSO and stored desiccated at -20 C in the dark.

name: Lipid-hydroperoxide and lipid-radical probes
plainEnglish: Reaction-based probes such as LiperFluo or lipid-radical probes report selected reactive lipid products in cells.
measures: Probe-dependent chemistry with lipid hydroperoxides or lipid-derived radicals.
cannotProve: A probe is a chemical participant, not a passive camera; signal does not equal unperturbed endogenous flux.
bestPractice: Define the probe reaction and compartment
Use dose and timing controls
Test whether the probe itself changes death
Validate with products or mass spectrometry

name: MDA, 4-HNE and bulk oxidation products
plainEnglish: Measure secondary products formed after lipid oxidation using chemical assays, immunoassays or targeted analysis.
measures: Bulk downstream products of oxidative lipid damage.
cannotProve: These products are not specific to ferroptosis and may appear after several types of injury.
bestPractice: Prefer chemically resolved LC–MS methods where possible
Collect before terminal nonspecific damage
Use orthogonal ferroptosis evidence
commonPitfalls: TBARS treated as a molecule-specific assay
Antibody signal treated as direct lipid identification

decisionProfile.provisionalBecause: 11 of 13 decision axes were read from Zou 2020 (the demonstrating study) on 2026-07-24; positiveControl and confounders are pending because the opened scopes do not establish them. Values record a demonstrating study's implementation, not a universal protocol.

specimen.value: In the demonstrating study (Zou 2020): the ferroptosis cell system read out in Fig. 5 (786-O clear-cell renal carcinoma), comparing control against POR-depleted cells.

evidence.claimFragment: the ferroptosis cell system read out in Fig. 5 (786-O clear-cell renal carcinoma)
evidence.supportNote: The MDA Methods give the assay protocol; the cell system and control/POR-depleted comparison are read from the Fig. 5 experiment the assay reports on.

evidence.claimFragment: comparing control against POR-depleted cells
evidence.supportNote: Fig. 5 is where the MDA readout is placed, against the POR-depletion arm.

question.value: Whether a bulk downstream lipid-peroxidation product (malondialdehyde) rises under ferroptotic stress and falls when the pathway is blocked.

evidence.claimFragment: a bulk downstream lipid-peroxidation product (malondialdehyde) rises under ferroptotic stress and falls when the pathway is blocked
evidence.supportNote: The POR-peroxidation results describe MDA rising with ferroptotic stress and falling on rescue.

perturbation.value: DMSO, ML210, or ML210 plus liproxstatin-1 for 90 minutes; POR depletion as the genetic arm.

evidence.claimFragment: DMSO, ML210, or ML210 plus liproxstatin-1 for 90 minutes
evidence.supportNote: The MDA Methods state the DMSO / ML210 / ML210+Lip-1 90-minute treatment and the POR-depletion genetic arm.

readout.value: The malondialdehyde-thiobarbituric-acid adduct, measured fluorometrically with a commercial kit (Abcam ab118970).

evidence.claimFragment: measured fluorometrically with a commercial kit (Abcam ab118970)
evidence.supportNote: The Methods name the Abcam ab118970 kit, the TBA addition and the fluorometric readout verbatim.

evidence.claimFragment: The malondialdehyde-thiobarbituric-acid adduct
evidence.supportNote: The kit reports a fluorescent TBARS signal; identifying that signal specifically as the MDA–TBA adduct is the standard reading of the assay, not a species identification the Methods make, so it is derived rather than explicit.

quantificationUnit.value: Fluorometric MDA signal read at excitation/emission 532/553 nm, normalised to protein concentration.

evidence.claimFragment: Fluorometric MDA signal read at excitation/emission 532/553 nm, normalised to protein concentration
evidence.supportNote: The Methods state Ex/Em 532/553 nm and protein normalisation.

instrument.value: A fluorescence microplate reader; sample protein quantified on a Qubit; a Dounce homogeniser for lysis.

evidence.claimFragment: A fluorescence microplate reader
evidence.supportNote: The Methods name the plate reader, Qubit protein quantification and Dounce homogenisation.

positiveControl.unresolved: The MDA Methods read do not name a defined MDA positive standard beyond the kit, and whether the kit's own MDA standard curve was run is not stated in the accessible text. Resolving it requires the kit protocol or the figure legend.

negativeControl.value: Liproxstatin-1 co-treated with ML210 as a ferroptosis-specific rescue comparator (the MDA rise is expected to fall if it is ferroptotic), with DMSO as the untreated baseline; both are matched conditions expected not to produce the ferroptotic MDA signal.

evidence.claimFragment: Liproxstatin-1 co-treated with ML210 as a ferroptosis-specific rescue comparator
evidence.supportNote: The Methods record the DMSO baseline, ML210 and ML210+Lip-1 rescue conditions verbatim.

evidence.claimFragment: DMSO baseline and the liproxstatin-1 rescue serve as the negative-control axis
evidence.supportNote: Assigning the baseline and rescue conditions to the negative-control axis is a classification per the control-axis definitions, not a label the Methods themselves apply.

processControl.value: Butylated hydroxytoluene (BHT) added to the lysis buffer to prevent ex vivo lipid oxidation during processing, and protein normalisation of every sample.

evidence.claimFragment: Butylated hydroxytoluene (BHT) added to the lysis buffer
evidence.supportNote: The Methods state that BHT is added to the lysis buffer and that signal is normalised to protein.

evidence.claimFragment: BHT functions as a process control that limits ex-vivo lipid oxidation during handling
evidence.supportNote: That BHT is present is explicit; reading its role as a process control against ex-vivo oxidation is the accepted rationale for the additive, classified here, not stated as such in the Methods.

orthogonalConfirmation.value: In the demonstrating study MDA is read together with BODIPY-C11 and species-resolved redox-lipidomics; the bulk product is corroborated by, not substituted for, the species-level measurement. This is what the study paired, not a universal requirement.

evidence.claimFragment: MDA is read together with BODIPY-C11 and species-resolved redox-lipidomics
evidence.supportNote: Fig. 5 shows the MDA readout together with BODIPY-C11 and redox-lipidomics.

evidence.claimFragment: the bulk product corroborates, rather than substitutes for, the species-level measurement
evidence.supportNote: The readouts appear together in the results; reading them as mutually corroborating is a derivation, not a statement that the study requires them to move together.

evidence.claimFragment: Acute: 90-minute treatment before collection
evidence.supportNote: The Methods state a 90-minute treatment before collection.

compartmentResolution.value: None: MDA is measured from a bulk cell homogenate, with no molecular-species or compartment resolution.

evidence.claimFragment: MDA is measured from a bulk cell homogenate
evidence.supportNote: The Methods measure MDA from a bulk homogenate workflow.

evidence.claimFragment: so the assay carries no molecular-species or compartment resolution
evidence.supportNote: The absence of species or compartment resolution follows from the bulk-homogenate design; it is an analytical inference, not a limitation the Methods state.

confounders.unresolved: That the thiobarbituric-acid reaction is non-specific - detecting a class of aldehydes and rising after several kinds of oxidative injury - is a general property of the TBARS assay, but the opened Zou MDA Methods paragraph does not state it, and this module's declared source (the 2025 field recommendation) was read only at abstract level. Resolving it requires an opened authoritative source that states the specificity boundary. Only the BHT/process-handling statement, which Zou does state, is kept, under processControl.

name: Oxidized phospholipid LC–MS/MS
plainEnglish: Separate and identify oxidized phospholipid species by mass, retention and fragmentation rather than measuring one bulk fluorescence signal.
measures: Specific oxidized lipid molecular species within the limits of extraction, chromatography, standards and fragmentation.
cannotProve: Association of one oxidized species with death does not establish that it is necessary or sufficient to execute death.
bestPractice: Use antioxidants and cold, rapid extraction to limit artefacts
Report internal standards and identification confidence
Separate isomers where the claim requires it
Connect molecules to genetic or rescue evidence
commonPitfalls: Relative peak areas presented as absolute concentration

specimen.value: In the demonstrating study (Kagan 2017): Pfa1 mouse embryonic fibroblasts (wild-type, Acsl4-knockout and inducible Gpx4-knockout), mouse lung epithelial (MLE) cells, and kidney from tamoxifen-inducible Gpx4-knockout mice.

evidence.claimFragment: Pfa1 mouse embryonic fibroblasts (wild-type, Acsl4-knockout and inducible Gpx4-knockout), mouse lung epithelial (MLE) cells
evidence.supportNote: The cell-lines Methods name the Pfa1, Acsl4-KO, Gpx4-KO and MLE systems.

evidence.claimFragment: kidney from tamoxifen-inducible Gpx4-knockout mice
evidence.supportNote: The Gpx4 KO mice Methods name the tamoxifen-inducible kidney model.

question.value: Which oxidised phospholipid molecular species accumulate specifically in ferroptotic cells and whether they track death rather than reporting bulk oxidation.

evidence.claimFragment: Which oxidised phospholipid molecular species accumulate specifically in ferroptotic cells
evidence.supportNote: The abstract frames the question of which oxygenated PE species drive ferroptosis.

evidence.claimFragment: whether they track death rather than reporting bulk oxidation
evidence.supportNote: The redox-phospholipidomics results pose the species-specific question.

perturbation.value: GPX4 inhibition by RSL3 (100 nM), genetic Gpx4 loss, ACSL4 loss, and arachidonic-acid or deuterated d8-arachidonic-acid supplementation; rosiglitazone to suppress AA/AdA esterification into PE.

evidence.claimFragment: GPX4 inhibition by RSL3 (100 nM), genetic Gpx4 loss, ACSL4 loss, and arachidonic-acid or deuterated d8-arachidonic-acid supplementation
evidence.supportNote: The Methods state RSL3 100 nM, Gpx4/ACSL4 loss, AA/d8-AA supplementation and rosiglitazone.

evidence.claimFragment: rosiglitazone to suppress AA/AdA esterification into PE
evidence.supportNote: The Methods describe rosiglitazone suppressing AA/AdA esterification into PE.

readout.value: Oxygenated phospholipid molecular species resolved by LC-MS/MS, with esterified oxidised fatty acids released by PAF-acetylhydrolase and species assigned by MS2/MS3 fragmentation (e.g. 15-hydroperoxy-AA-PE and its di- and tri-oxygenated forms).

evidence.claimFragment: Oxygenated phospholipid molecular species resolved by LC-MS/MS
evidence.supportNote: The identification Methods resolve oxygenated phospholipid species by LC-MS/MS.

evidence.claimFragment: esterified oxidised fatty acids released by PAF-acetylhydrolase
evidence.supportNote: The free/esterified LC-MS Methods use PAF-AH to release esterified oxidised acyls.

evidence.claimFragment: species assigned by MS2/MS3 fragmentation
evidence.supportNote: Fig. 5 shows the species assigned by MS2/MS3 fragmentation.

evidence.claimFragment: 15-hydroperoxy-AA-PE and its di- and tri-oxygenated forms
evidence.supportNote: Fig. 6 shows the di-/tri-oxygenated forms.

quantificationUnit.value: Esterified oxygenated acyls in pmol per micromole of phospholipid (e.g. 199.3 +/- 26.2 pmol/micromole in wild-type against 72.2 +/- 27.0 in Acsl4-knockout); phospholipids quantified ratiometrically against a pre-selected internal standard using a per-class standard curve.

evidence.claimFragment: Esterified oxygenated acyls in pmol per micromole of phospholipid
evidence.supportNote: The results give the pmol/micromole figures for wild-type against Acsl4-KO.

evidence.claimFragment: phospholipids quantified ratiometrically against a pre-selected internal standard using a per-class standard curve
evidence.supportNote: The Statistical Analysis Methods state ratiometric comparison to a pre-selected internal standard using a standard curve for each phospholipid class.

instrument.value: Thermo Fisher Orbitrap-class mass spectrometry: Q-Exactive hybrid quadrupole-Orbitrap for phospholipid and fatty-acid MS/MS, an Orbitrap Fusion Lumos for data-dependent MS2/targeted-MS3 structural work, and an LXQ ion trap for purity confirmation; normal-phase silica and reverse-phase C18/C8 HPLC upstream.

evidence.claimFragment: Q-Exactive hybrid quadrupole-Orbitrap for phospholipid and fatty-acid MS/MS, an Orbitrap Fusion Lumos for data-dependent MS2/targeted-MS3 structural work, and an LXQ ion trap for purity confirmation
evidence.supportNote: The MS Methods name the Q-Exactive, Orbitrap Fusion Lumos and LXQ instruments.

evidence.claimFragment: normal-phase silica and reverse-phase C18/C8 HPLC upstream
evidence.supportNote: The Methods describe the normal-phase and reverse-phase HPLC separation.

positiveControl.value: Authentic SAPE-OOH (1-stearoyl-2-arachidonoyl-PE hydroperoxide), prepared by 15-lipoxygenase oxidation and HPLC-purified to 99%, run as an analytical identity/reference standard to confirm that the oxygenated-PE species detected in cells match the authentic hydroperoxide. It verifies the assay's positive signal at the level of species identity; it is an analytical reference standard, not a biological positive-control condition.

evidence.claimFragment: prepared by 15-lipoxygenase oxidation and HPLC-purified to 99%
evidence.supportNote: The Methods prepare and HPLC-purify authentic SAPE-OOH to 99%.

evidence.claimFragment: it serves as the assay's positive-control / analytical identity-reference axis
evidence.supportNote: The authentic standard is used to confirm the identity of the oxygenated PE detected in cells; classifying it as the positive-control axis is a control-axis assignment, not a term the Methods use.

negativeControl.value: Acsl4-knockout cells as a matched genetic condition in which the oxygenated-PE signal is strongly reduced (199.3 pmol/micromole in wild-type against 72.2 in Acsl4-knockout), and the non-oxygenated precursor phospholipids measured in parallel as a within-run reference expected not to carry the ferroptotic oxygenation signal.

evidence.claimFragment: the oxygenated-PE signal is strongly reduced
evidence.supportNote: The Acsl4-KO results show the oxygenated-PE signal strongly reduced when the substrate-generating enzyme is lost.

evidence.claimFragment: Acsl4-knockout is read as the assay's matched genetic negative-control condition
evidence.supportNote: The reduction is explicit; treating Acsl4-KO as the negative-control axis is a classification per the control-axis definitions.

processControl.value: Phospholipids quantified ratiometrically against a pre-selected internal standard using a per-class standard curve, which controls analytical quantification performance across phospholipid classes.

evidence.claimFragment: Phospholipids quantified ratiometrically against a pre-selected internal standard using a per-class standard curve
evidence.supportNote: The Statistical Analysis Methods state ratiometric comparison to a pre-selected internal standard with a per-class standard curve.

evidence.claimFragment: this internal-standard quantification is read as the analytical process control
evidence.supportNote: The quantification scheme is explicit; naming it the process control that governs analytical performance is a control-axis classification, not a statement the Methods make.

orthogonalConfirmation.value: MS2/MS3 fragmentation for structural assignment, stable-isotope d8-arachidonic-acid tracing to establish the precursor route, and convergent genetic (Acsl4 knockout) and pharmacological (rosiglitazone) suppression of the same species.

evidence.claimFragment: MS2/MS3 fragmentation for structural assignment
evidence.supportNote: The identification results use MS2/MS3 and d8-AA tracing verbatim.

evidence.claimFragment: convergent genetic (Acsl4-KO) and pharmacological (rosiglitazone) suppression of the same species tests the conclusion by another principle
evidence.supportNote: The individual suppressions are explicit; reading them as orthogonal confirmation of one biological conclusion is a derivation.

evidence.claimFragment: stable-isotope d8-arachidonic-acid tracing to establish the precursor route
evidence.supportNote: The identification results use d8-AA tracing to establish the precursor route.

timing.value: Acute: RSL3 at 100 nM for 6 hours in cells (MLE at 0.5-1.0 micromolar); in vivo, kidney sampled 8 days after inducible Gpx4 knockout.

evidence.claimFragment: RSL3 at 100 nM for 6 hours in cells (MLE at 0.5-1.0 micromolar)
evidence.supportNote: The Methods state RSL3 100 nM for 6 h (MLE 0.5-1.0 uM).

evidence.claimFragment: kidney sampled 8 days after inducible Gpx4 knockout
evidence.supportNote: The kidney results state sampling 8 days after inducible Gpx4 knockout.

compartmentResolution.value: None within the mass spectrometry itself: species are measured from a whole-cell (or whole-tissue) lipid extract. The endoplasmic-reticulum localisation reported by the paper came from a separate LiperFluo live-imaging experiment, not from the LC-MS.

evidence.claimFragment: the LC-MS measures species from a whole-cell or whole-tissue lipid extract, so it carries no compartment resolution
evidence.supportNote: The whole-extract LC-MS preparation is explicit; that it therefore resolves no compartment is an analytical inference from the extraction design. The absence of spatial resolution is cited to the extraction/LC-MS scope, not to the imaging scopes.

evidence.claimFragment: came from a separate LiperFluo live-imaging experiment
evidence.supportNote: The Live Cell Imaging Methods establish the ER localisation through LiperFluo imaging, which is a different experiment from the LC-MS.

evidence.claimFragment: not from the LC-MS
evidence.supportNote: The LiperFluo ER-accumulation result establishes only where the paper obtained its ER localisation; it does not give the LC-MS spatial resolution.

confounders.value: Explicit procedural facts: esterified oxidised acyls are read only after release by PAF-acetylhydrolase, and detection uses targeted MS inclusion lists that see only the species they are set for. Analytical inference: because the inclusion lists are targeted, an undetected species is not evidence of absence.

evidence.claimFragment: esterified oxidised acyls are read only after release by PAF-acetylhydrolase
evidence.supportNote: The Methods state that esterified oxidised acyls are released by PAF-acetylhydrolase before analysis.

evidence.claimFragment: detection uses targeted MS inclusion lists that see only the species they are set for
evidence.supportNote: The identification Methods state the use of targeted MS inclusion lists.

evidence.claimFragment: an undetected species is therefore not evidence of absence
evidence.supportNote: That an undetected species is not evidence of absence follows from the targeted inclusion-list design; it is analytical inference, not a stated limitation.

Test probe or chelator perturbation

name: Autoxidation and radical-trapping kinetics
plainEnglish: Measure how fast compounds react with chain-carrying lipid radicals in defined solutions or membranes.
measures: Rate constants, inhibition periods and stoichiometry under a specified chemical environment.
cannotProve: A rate measured in homogeneous solvent cannot be transferred directly to a cellular membrane or organism.
bestPractice: Report solvent, lipid phase, initiator and oxygen conditions
Compare membrane and nonpolar environments
Connect chemical potency with cellular exposure
commonPitfalls: DPPH used as a ferroptosis predictor
Equating antioxidant chemistry with target specificity

name: Organelle-resolved and live-cell imaging
plainEnglish: Follow lipid oxidation, iron, membrane integrity or cell-to-cell propagation over time and within subcellular regions.
measures: Probe- and segmentation-defined spatial and temporal signals.
cannotProve: Targeted probes or forced organelle perturbations show local feasibility, not necessarily the endogenous starting site.
bestPractice: Record inducer, cell type, probe, compartment and time window as one condition vector
Validate organelle assignment
Measure whether the probe perturbs the process
Use temporal order plus necessity tests
commonPitfalls: Overstating an organelle as the universal origin
